stories.title: Kasidi Pickering Signs with Texas Tech — Three All-Americans Complete Historic Class
stories.angle: Oklahoma All-American Kasidi Pickering (.370 BA / 20 HR / 61 RBI / career .384 / 50 HR / 169 RBI) has officially signed with Texas Tech, completing what is widely regarded as the most dominant transfer portal class in college softball history. The Red Raiders now have three All-Americans: Moriah Polar (nation's top hitter, .555 BA / 101 hits), Jayden Heavener (No. 1 portal pitcher, 2.81 ERA / 128 K), and Pickering. Softball America ranks the Texas Tech class No. 1 nationally.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
IT'S OFFICIAL.

Kasidi Pickering — career .384 BA, 50 HR, 169 RBI — has signed with Texas Tech.

Texas Tech now has THREE All-Americans: Polar (.555 BA), Heavener, Pickering (20 HR).

Softball America's No. 1 class. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech's 2027 portal class:

✅ Moriah Polar — .555 BA, nation's top hitter
✅ Jayden Heavener — No. 1 pitcher in the portal
✅ Kasidi Pickering — 50 career HR, 2025 All-American

Softball America calls it the No. 1 class in the country. Hard to argue. #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
The Texas Tech Red Raiders just completed the most dominant transfer portal class in college softball history.

Kasidi Pickering — a career .384 hitter with 50 home runs and 169 RBIs in three seasons at Oklahoma — has officially signed with Texas Tech. She was one of the most decorated players in Oklahoma softball history, a 2025 NFCA All-American who helped the Sooners win a national championship in 2024. Now she's heading to Lubbock with one year of eligibility and a very specific goal.

Here's the Texas Tech 2026 portal class, now complete:

🔴 Moriah Polar — .555 batting average, 101 hits, nation's top hitter (from Purdue)
🔴 Jayden Heavener — No. 1 pitcher in the portal, 2.81 ERA, 128 strikeouts
🔴 Kasidi Pickering — career .384 BA, 50 HR, 169 RBI (from Oklahoma)

Softball America has ranked the Red Raiders' class No. 1 nationally. Every major outlet covering college softball agrees: no program added more value in this 15-day window than Texas Tech.

The Red Raiders finished 61-8 in 2026 and came within one game of winning it all, losing to Texas in the WCWS championship series. They return a core that knows what the final stage feels like. They've now added three All-Americans to that experience.

Texas won the title. Texas Tech built the class.

In 2027, the rematch is coming.
facebook.image_caption: Texas Tech just signed Kasidi Pickering — career .384 BA, 50 HR, 169 RBI — to complete a three-All-American portal class. Polar. Heavener. Pickering. Softball America's No. 1 class in the country. The Red Raiders are coming for the championship they couldn't win in 2026. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Beachum Watch — Day 11, Four Days Left and Still Uncommitted
stories.angle: Jaysoni Beachum (.417 BA / 16 HR / 66 RBI / .549 OBP / career 37 HR / 180 RBI / 202 hits at FSU) is the last elite transfer still on the board. Softball America No. 1. With Pickering now confirmed for Texas Tech, Beachum stands as the single most coveted uncommitted player in the portal. Four days remain before the June 22 close. No program has been publicly confirmed as the frontrunner.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Pickering is gone. Torres is gone. Polar is gone.

One elite name remains in the transfer portal: Jaysoni Beachum.

Softball America No. 1. .417 BA. 16 HR. 66 RBI. Career 37 HR, 180 RBI.

Four days left. Who's getting her? #SoftballTwitter

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The last big card in the 2026 portal: Jaysoni Beachum.

Day 11. Four days to June 22 close.

2024 NFCA Freshman of the Year. 2026 Second-Team All-American. 37 career HRs at FSU.

Every power program still standing is watching. #SoftballTwitter

facebook.long_form:
Kasidi Pickering is gone. Isa Torres is gone. Moriah Polar is gone.

The 2026 transfer portal has given away its stars one by one, and now there is one name left: Jaysoni Beachum.

The Florida State third baseman — Softball America's No. 1 transfer, the 2024 NFCA Freshman of the Year, and a 2026 Second-Team All-American — is entering Day 11 of the 15-day portal window still uncommitted. Four days remain before the June 22 deadline.

What makes Beachum special is everything about her profile. At FSU over three seasons, she produced a .375 batting average, 37 home runs, 180 RBIs, and 202 hits. In 2026 alone, she posted a .417 average with 16 home runs, 66 RBIs, and a .549 on-base percentage. She is a force at the plate who fits into any elite lineup.

The question everyone is asking: where does she go?

With Texas and Texas Tech both fully loaded from this portal cycle, the calculus is interesting. Beachum to one of those programs would make them even more formidable. Beachum to a program like Alabama, Florida, or Oklahoma could instantly transform their 2027 outlook.

No school has emerged as a confirmed frontrunner. No announcement has been posted. Day 11 ends without a resolution, and the four days ahead may be the most watched stretch of this entire portal window.
facebook.image_caption: Day 11. Four days left. Jaysoni Beachum — Softball America's No. 1 transfer, .417 BA, 37 career HRs at FSU — is still uncommitted. The portal closes June 22. This is the last big move of the 2026 cycle. #SoftballTwitter

stories.title: Texas Tech's Historic Portal Class — What Polar + Heavener + Pickering Mean for 2027
stories.angle: With Pickering signed, Texas Tech's three-All-American portal class is formally complete. Assessing what it means: WCWS runner-up adding nation's top hitter, No. 1 portal pitcher, and a power bat with 50 career HRs. Softball America and D1Softball both call it the best portal class in the sport's history. The Red Raiders were 61-8 in 2026. They just got demonstrably better.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Texas went 2-for-2 in the WCWS championship. Texas Tech just went 3-for-3 in the portal.

Polar. Heavener. Pickering.

Nation's top hitter. No. 1 pitcher. 50-HR power bat.

Texas vs. Texas Tech in 2027 might be the best rivalry in softball history. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Take a step back for a moment and consider what Texas Tech just did in 15 days.

The Red Raiders — who finished the 2026 season at 61-8 and fell to Texas in the WCWS championship game — entered the transfer portal window as a program with unfinished business. They left it with the most powerful collection of incoming transfer talent in the history of the sport.

Three All-Americans:

🔴 Moriah Polar (Purdue): .555 batting average, 101 hits. She was the best hitter in college softball by batting average in 2026 — the kind of contact hitter who doesn't just affect individual at-bats but changes how an entire lineup is pitched.

🔴 Jayden Heavener: Ranked No. 1 among portal pitchers by D1Softball. A 2.81 ERA and 128 strikeouts. She adds another elite arm to a staff that already made it to the national championship game.

🔴 Kasidi Pickering (Oklahoma): Career .384 batting average. Fifty home runs. One hundred sixty-nine RBIs in three seasons with the Sooners. A 2025 NFCA All-American with a national championship ring. A power bat returning to her home state of Texas.

Softball America ranks this class No. 1 in the country. It is not a close call.

Texas won the 2026 title with two-time MOP Teagan Kavan and a dominant roster built over years. Texas Tech built a response in two weeks.

The 2027 Texas-Texas Tech matchup — whenever it comes — will be one for history.
facebook.image_caption: Polar. Heavener. Pickering. Texas Tech's portal class is complete — and it's the No. 1 class in the country according to Softball America. The WCWS runner-up just got three All-Americans. 2027 is going to be something else. #WreckEm #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Tennessee Adds Karley Shelton, Mardjetko Returns — Lady Vols Reload
stories.angle: Tennessee lands South Carolina transfer Karley Shelton (.345 AVG / 68 H / 40 RBI / 108 TB) for the 2027 infield. Simultaneously, ace pitcher Sage Mardjetko announces she is returning to Rocky Top for her senior season. The Lady Vols, who reached the WCWS semifinals in 2026, are retooling efficiently with complementary portal additions after losing three players to the portal themselves.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tennessee isn't done building.

✅ Karley Shelton (SC transfer, .345 BA, 40 RBI) joins the 2027 infield
✅ Sage Mardjetko is returning for her senior season

The Lady Vols went to the WCWS semis in 2026. They're not finished. #GBO #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
While the biggest transfer portal headlines belonged to Texas and Texas Tech, Tennessee quietly made some significant moves of its own.

The Lady Vols — who reached the WCWS semifinals in 2026 — added South Carolina transfer Karley Shelton to the 2027 roster. Shelton, a rising senior, batted .345 with 68 hits, 40 RBIs, and 108 total bases in her junior season with the Gamecocks. She played second base for South Carolina and brings proven production to what should be a strengthened Tennessee infield.

And then there was the news that many Lady Vols fans were waiting for: Sage Mardjetko is coming back.

The ace pitcher, who was a key figure in Tennessee's 2026 postseason run — including a clutch performance in the WCWS — announced she will return to Rocky Top for her senior season. Mardjetko's return means Tennessee retains the veteran pitching anchor around whom the 2027 rotation can be built.

Tennessee did lose three players to the portal this cycle: outfielder Saviya Morgan, pitcher Kailey Plumlee (who committed to Alabama), and infielder Ella Dodge. But the combination of Shelton joining and Mardjetko staying suggests a program that is navigating the portal era with both give and take — and coming out ahead.
facebook.image_caption: Tennessee adds South Carolina transfer Karley Shelton (.345 BA, 40 RBI) for the 2027 infield — and ace Sage Mardjetko is returning for her senior season. The Lady Vols went to the WCWS semis in 2026. They're building for more. #GBO #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Portal Countdown — Day 11 of 15, Four Days Remain
stories.angle: Day 11 of the 15-day transfer portal window. Four days remain before the June 22 close. Confirmed/signed: Torres (Texas), Swan (Texas), Lincoln (Texas), Polar (Texas Tech), Heavener (Texas Tech), Pickering (Texas Tech), Hartley (OSU), Wetteland (Arkansas), Azua (Texas A&M), Plumlee (Alabama), Altmeyer (Texas), Shelton (Tennessee). Still available: Beachum (No. 1 remaining, Softball America). Texas and Texas Tech are fully loaded. The final chapter belongs to Beachum.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Transfer portal: Day 11 of 15. Four days left.

Texas loaded. Texas Tech loaded. 12 major transfers committed.

One name remains: Beachum (Softball America No. 1).

The window closes June 22. The final move of this portal could be the biggest. #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
Day 11 of the 2026 NCAA softball transfer portal. Four days remain before the window closes on June 22.

Here is where things stand:

✅ **Committed/Signed:**
- Isa Torres → Texas (D1 POY, .530 BA)
- Samantha Swan → Texas (No. 1 catcher, .383 BA)
- Samantha Lincoln → Texas (pitcher, ex-Texas Tech)
- Moriah Polar → Texas Tech (.555 BA, No. 1 hitter)
- Jayden Heavener → Texas Tech (No. 1 pitcher, 2.81 ERA)
- Kasidi Pickering → Texas Tech (career .384 BA, 50 HR)
- Karley Shelton → Tennessee (.345 BA, 40 RBI)
- Hannah Hartley → Oklahoma State
- Taylor Wetteland → Arkansas
- Maddy Azua → Texas A&M
- Kailey Plumlee → Alabama
- Savannah Altmeyer → Texas

⏳ **Still Available (Major):**
- Jaysoni Beachum (FSU, SA No. 1) — uncommitted

The two programs that dominated this window — Texas and Texas Tech — are done building. The last major card in this cycle is Beachum. Whoever lands her ends a remarkable 15-day stretch with one final exclamation point.

Four days. One name. The portal's final chapter is still being written.
facebook.image_caption: Day 11 of 15. 12 major transfers committed. Texas has three All-Americans. Texas Tech has three All-Americans. One name still on the board: Jaysoni Beachum, Softball America No. 1. The portal closes June 22. #CollegeSoftball
